title: Moods, Emotions and Feelings
justification: This talk provides the most complete and coherent framework for the chapter theme, systematically exploring how desire for sensual pleasure generates the full spectrum of moods, emotions, and feelings. It offers exceptional literary quality through the memorable 'Little Lily and the ice cream' extended narrative, which illustrates the psychological rounds of desire, denial, bargaining, anger, and depression with vivid storytelling. The talk's clear structure—moving from definitions through psychological mechanisms to spiritual resolution—creates an ideal spine for grafting complementary material.
summary: This teaching explores how desire for sensual pleasure creates our emotional suffering, using the extended metaphor of a child wanting ice cream to illuminate the psychological patterns of craving, frustration, and attachment. It culminates in showing how meditation allows us to experience these states objectively, ultimately freeing ourselves from the burden of obsessive desire.

relevance.note: Primarily about sensual desires creating emotional patterns and suffering
